channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Baynes Street Canalside Garden, 5th March
item.description:
The trees in the garden are encroaching on the southern aspect of Reachview Close and we have just discovered some foundation concerns which could be the result of the size and density of the trees in the garden.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Regent's Canal towpath, Kentish Town, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:
Councillor report. As above

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): William Booth Road, Crystal Palace, Greater London

item.title: Blue bins not collected for past 2 wweks, 5th March
item.description:
The blue bins have not been collected for the past 2 weeks. This is an urgent matter. Has been raised to the council today but no response in collecting.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Bassant Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Looks like roof or floor insulation material, 5th March
item.description:
Photographs were taken on Tuesday 17th February at noon and the refuse was still there on Tuesday 3rd March

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Sheepcote Lane, St Mary Cray, Greater London

item.title: Lots of broken glass on road outside 13A Cambridge rd, 5th March
item.description:
Lots of broken glass (about 3 bottles I think) on road outside 13A Cambridge rd. Since this morning.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cambridge Road, Plaistow, Greater London

item.title: Bed frame, mattress and bedding, 5th March
item.description:
Dumped by local residents, no anti fly tipping signage or investigations into perpetrators.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 9-19, Raul Road, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Shrewsbury Park Boundary Fence - Partial Targeted 'Repair', 5th March
item.description:
Earlier today (March 5th), a partial fence repair took place at the rear of my property. This was not requested by me. The remaining boundary fence in the park has many areas in much need of repair but these have all been ignored? Please return to repair the full fence, rather than specifically targeting the rear of our house and ignoring others. A council fence panel at our back garden lay broken for a very long time. We made use of inserting a gate into our own fence,(an additional secure rear fence that was previously required because the council fence was falling apart), in order to assist with easier access to the park with our elderly dog and young baby. Seemingly, this must have offended someone. If the fence is being repaired then please repair all areas that are broken, rather than specifically targeting our household to stop us entering the park safely via our back garden. The limited targeted repair seems vindictive and spiteful against our family and our enjoyment of our home, garden and surrounding park amenities. We had made an improvement to the eyesore, but now it looks messy again because only some bits have been replaced and others ignored. Was a repair budget approved for only a small portion of a park boundary fence? - and why when there is damage across most of the fence length? Why was the rear of our property suddenly targeted for urgent need of a limited repair? The broken council panel remained there but wasn't even used in the repair so material was also needlessly wasted.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Ashridge Crescent, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandonned bike, 5th March
item.description:
This bike has been here for 3 weeks People are using it as a public bin PLEASE REMOTE IT ASAP

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Londesborough Road, Hackney, London
